Blood-flow direction inference to determine position of victim
Microscopic analysis of textile fibres to link crime scene to murder location
Reconstruction of broken spectacle lenses to detect planted glass
Examination of boot soles to determine whether victim walked to crime scene

Pipe physiognomy analysis to identify victim and establish exchange
Microscopic identification of mole hairs to confirm pipe ownership
Analysis of acorn barnacle and serpula fragments in scalp wound to locate crime scene

Detection of forged finger-prints via thumb-plane impossibility and scar comparison

Brodski's broken spectacle lenses with planted extra glass fragments

Forged finger-print stamps made from Belfield's official prison form
Dockhead, Bermondsey (Bailey's flat at Hanover Buildings)
Norwegian knife smeared with rat blood and musk essence
Thumbograph (finger-print album) used in Hornby case

Third finger of left hand missing from skeleton; removed for ring
John Bellingham's body concealed inside Sebek-hotep cartonnage at the British Museum
Scattered bones are parts of Sebek-hotep mummy, not Bellingham
Will clause 2 conditions burial in St. George Bloomsbury parishes; accidentally fulfilled by Museum deposit
Survivorship: last proven moment Bellingham was alive determines property succession
X-ray / skiagraph bone analysis of mummy
Dental record comparison for mummy identification
Systematic skeletal examination (measurements, eburnation, adipocere, submersion marks)

Eye of Osiris ring (18th dynasty copy, worn on left ring finger)
Lapis lazuli scarab (18th dynasty, Amenhotep III cartouche) on watch-chain

Silver suture wires in both kneecaps (surgical repair by Sir Morgan Bennet)
Eburnation (rheumatoid arthritis patch) on right hip-joint
Snails' eggs and worm-tubes on bones showing submersion position and separate hand
Absence of adipocere proving dry-atmosphere decay (mummy bones, not fresh remains)
Ring and mummy finger-bones found in well at Woodford house
Gold and tin dental fillings identified in mummy X-ray matching Bellingham's dentist records
Jellicoe's financial motive: assigned share in Hurst's inheritance (£50,000)
